_note: Updated daily at noon by the strategy agent. Plain language — no jargon.

priorities.title: Establish our position as the go-to AI-native game dev tool before the window closes
priorities.note: We have roughly 18 to 36 months before well-funded competitors crowd this space. The plan is to be the established name before that happens — not try to fight our way in after.
priorities.proposed_solution: Ship visible proof of an AI-native, full-pipeline toolchain narrative early while the competitive window is still open.

priorities.title: Define what 'good enough to charge for' looks like for the AI room assistant
priorities.note: We want to launch as a paid tool when the AI features are clearly better than anything else. No one has defined the bar yet. That needs to happen.
priorities.proposed_solution: Partner with Analytics and Engineering to define measurable “good enough to charge for” Copilot criteria the founder can approve.

priorities.title: Lock the scope of Ashen Hollow so it can actually be finished
priorities.note: An unfinished game breaks the whole argument for the tools. We need a hard ceiling on what v1.0 of the game contains — biomes, rooms, mechanics — and a founder decision to commit to it.
priorities.proposed_solution: Facilitate a founder decision that caps Ashen Hollow v1.0 content and records scope explicitly in the risk register.

risks_p1.title: No one has defined what makes the AI assistant good enough to charge for
risks_p1.note: Without a clear bar, the commercial launch keeps getting pushed back. This is a real risk of indefinite delay.

risks_p1.title: Ashen Hollow has no agreed scope ceiling
risks_p1.note: Scope creep on the game is the most likely thing to break the whole strategy. A game that never ships proves nothing.

founder_decisions.title: What does the AI assistant need to do before we charge for it?
founder_decisions.note: What does 'clearly better than anything else' look like in practice? We need a measurable answer, not a feeling.

founder_decisions.title: What's in Ashen Hollow v1.0 — and what isn't?
founder_decisions.note: How many biomes, rooms, and mechanics make a shippable first version? This needs a decision, not a roadmap.

founder_decisions.title: Authorize a time-boxed assurance-copilot spike—or keep it parked?
founder_decisions.note: Other-income idea: AI codegen assurance-by-process. Spike needs a week budget, one vertical, a reachable customer signal, and Legal/Finance gates. Default per 2026-04-02 decision: stay parked unless you say otherwise.

risk_register.assumption: There's a real market for AI-native game development tools
risk_register.risk_if_wrong: Not enough people want this to build a business around it
risk_register.monitor: Watch for funded competitors entering the space — if they're coming, the market is real

risk_register.assumption: AI models will keep getting better at spatial tasks like room layout
risk_register.risk_if_wrong: The AI assistant stays a novelty rather than becoming genuinely useful
risk_register.monitor: Test each major new model release against room layout quality

risk_register.assumption: One person can finish Ashen Hollow
risk_register.risk_if_wrong: The game never ships, which breaks the proof that the tools work
risk_register.monitor: Quarterly scope reviews — ruthless about cutting

risk_register.assumption: A browser-based tool is good enough for serious pixel art work
risk_register.risk_if_wrong: Professional users hit a performance wall and leave
risk_register.monitor: Watch why users stop using the tool if and when that data exists

risk_register.assumption: No big company builds a competing all-in-one AI game dev tool before we establish ourselves
risk_register.risk_if_wrong: Adobe, Unity, or Epic enters the space and outspends us
risk_register.monitor: Check major player announcements each quarter

metrics.revenue_model: Free to use, pay for AI features

competitors.name: Aseprite
competitors.note: Great pixel art editor but no level design, no AI, no full pipeline. Different ceiling.

competitors.name: LDtk
competitors.note: The best free level editor out there, but no sprite creation and no AI. Solves one piece of the puzzle.

competitors.name: Godot editor
competitors.note: Watch how its built-in tools improve. If they get good enough, some users won't need a separate tool.

competitors.name: AI-native tools (emerging)
competitors.note: No one has cracked integrated AI game dev tooling yet. The window to be first is 18 to 36 months. No new entrants spotted this quarter.

actions.name: Strategic Review
actions.description: Review all strategic assumptions — what held, what was falsified, what changed — and update the risk register.
actions.output_location: Assurance-copilot opportunity closure: decisions/2026-04-02-assurance-copilot-other-income-gate.md and strategy-status.json opportunity str-o-1775106803755 (full quarterly review not run)

actions.name: Risk Register Review
actions.description: Sweep the risk register, update the status of each assumption, add new risks, close resolved ones.
actions.trigger: Quarterly or after any major market event

actions.name: Competitive Window Assessment
actions.description: Update the estimate of the competitive window with current evidence for and against the 18-36 month thesis.
actions.trigger: Quarterly or when a new AI game tool competitor appears

actions.name: Competitor Deep Dive
actions.description: Assess a specific competitor or new entrant — positioning overlap, feature comparison, founder decision implications.
actions.trigger: When a new entrant is spotted or an existing competitor ships a significant feature

opportunities.title: Plan the right time to release a desktop app version
opportunities.note: The browser-first approach is working. A desktop version comes after we've proven the web product, not before.
opportunities.proposed_solution: Defer desktop packaging until the web product shows retention and economics worth the extra distribution cost.

opportunities.title: Plan when to build an engine plugin for Godot
opportunities.note: Makes the tools much stickier with a real user base. A phase two move — only after the saved file format is stable and enough people are using the tools.
opportunities.proposed_solution: Treat a Godot plugin as phase two after export schema stability and real user demand justify the integration effort.

opportunities.title: Business plan: quarterly refresh of assumptions and competitors
opportunities.note: Next step from the initial business plan (Apr 2026). Same work as strategic review and risk register review — keep dates honest in metrics.
opportunities.proposed_solution: Run strategic-review plus risk-register-review each quarter; update risk_register, competitors, and metrics.last_quarterly_review.

opportunities.title: Business Idea: Coding Agent that adopts safety or mission critical software development practices
opportunities.summary: Strategy execution completed 2026-04-02. Positioning: assurance-by-process for AI codegen (traceability, evidence, CI gates)—not DO-178C compliance claims. Product track stays deferred; only a time-boxed spike after explicit founder priority plus Legal and Finance gates.
opportunities.note: Research filing documents real demand and serious competition (lifecycle AI, verification tools, ALM). Credibility is not a moat. Default remains no standalone product. Decision log: decisions/2026-04-02-assurance-copilot-other-income-gate.md. Technical write-up: research/library/technical/assurance-copilot-do178c-market-2026-04-02.md.
opportunities.proposed_solution: If the founder authorizes a spike: time-box (e.g. 2–4 weeks), pick one vertical (avionics OR medtech OR auto), and define pass/fail—reachable first customer or LOI, Legal/Finance templates or budget, and a CAC or cycle-length sanity check. Otherwise stay paused.
opportunities.solution: Assurance-by-process: process discipline, traceability, provenance, and verification gates for AI-generated code—useful beyond safety-critical domains. Never market as DO-178C compliant or as a qualified tool without customer program context and legal sign-off.
open_questions: Founder: authorize a bounded spike now (yes/no) and week budget?
Can the founder name one reachable first customer or LOI path in a single vertical?
Prefer internal dogfooding on MV agent workflows before any external offer?
opportunities.internal_use_assessment: 2026-04-02. MV already uses lightweight assurance (charters, Truthfulness directive, decisions folder, dashboard output fields). Gaps are mostly enforcement (prompt-level vs automated CI). Next internal step: tighten agent completion hygiene; let that inform whether external product work is justified.
